sections.paragraph:
ὀθόνιον, ου, τό (Aristoph., Hippocr. et al.; ins [e.g. the Rosetta Stone: OGI 90, 18 (196 b.c.)]; pap [e.g. UPZ 85, 8; 42–163/160 b.c.]; Judg 14:13 B; Hos 2:7, 11; EpArist 320; JosAs 13:5 cod. A [p. 57, 11 Bat.]. Cp. O. Wilck I p. 266ff. On the origin of the word s. HLewy, Die semit. Fremdwörter im Griech. 1895, 124f; Thumb 111; on the flax plant s. Zohary, Plants 78, Geobot. II 628) dim. of ὀθόνη but not necessarily w. dim. force in our lit.; (linen) cloth, cloth wrapping J 19:40; 20:5, 6, 7; Lk 24:12. The applicability of the sense bandage (UPZ 85, 8; PGiss 68, 11) to our lit. is questionable. S. AVaccari, in Miscellanea biblica, BUbach ’53, 375–86, w. ref. to PRyl 627, 9 (IV a.d.]. S. also JBlinzler, ΟΘΟΝΙΑ etc.: Philol 99, ’55, 158–66; RBrown, AB: John 942 and JFitzmyer, AB: Luke 1548.—DELG s.v. ὀθόνη. M-M. Spicq.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

  1. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4

Thumb Thumb = ATh., Die griechische Sprache im Zeitalter des Hellenismus—List 6

Zohary Zohary = MZ., various works—List 6

dim. dim. = diminutive

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

ref. ref. = reference(s)

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

Philol Philol , Zeitschrift für das klassische Altertum—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 693
